title: eriolaena sterculiacea
intro: Eriolaena sterculiacea, also known as the forest flower or coral tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Eriolaena sterculiacea include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be applied topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
